_targets: clear/unambiguous case — anchors the basic format and an obvious bug_defect + negative + high urgency reading
input: The app crashes every single time I try to upload a photo. Completely unusable for my workflow.

output.key_quote: The app crashes every single time I try to upload a photo.

_targets: clear/unambiguous case — anchors praise_positive with no actionable ask
input: Just wanted to say the new dashboard redesign looks fantastic and loads so much faster. Great work.

output.key_quote: the new dashboard redesign looks fantastic and loads so much faster

_targets: boundary: performance vs bug_defect — slowness/timeouts are performance, not a logic defect
input: The reports page takes over a minute to load every time, sometimes it just times out completely.

output.key_quote: takes over a minute to load every time, sometimes it just times out completely

_targets: boundary: multi-issue feedback — primary chosen by actionability, secondary captures the rest, without polluting the primary distribution
input: The app is really slow on my phone lately, and when I looked in the help docs for a fix it didn't even mention this issue.

output.key_quote: really slow on my phone lately

_targets: boundary: feature_request vs bug_defect — a request phrased politely is still a bug if it describes broken behavior
input: It would be nice if the app didn't log me out randomly every time I switch tabs, that shouldn't be happening.

urgency_signals: disrupts session continuity across normal usage
output.key_quote: logs me out randomly every time I switch tabs

_targets: hard case: sarcasm — positive-sounding words expressing real frustration must be read as negative
input: Oh wonderful, another outage right in the middle of our biggest sale of the year. Great timing, really.

urgency_signals: occurring during a high-stakes business event
output.key_quote: another outage right in the middle of our biggest sale of the year

_targets: hard case: negation — 'wasn't bad' flips the literal negative word into a positive reading
input: Support actually wasn't bad this time around, got a quick and genuinely helpful reply within the hour.

output.key_quote: quick and genuinely helpful reply within the hour

_targets: hard case: mixed sentiment — genuinely two-sided feedback should use mixed, not be forced to one polarity
input: I love the new interface, it's so much cleaner, but checkout is completely broken and it cost me a sale today.

output.key_quote: checkout is completely broken and it cost me a sale today

_targets: critical urgency: security/privacy exposure — always flagged critical even with a calm tone
input: I noticed today that I could briefly see another customer's invoice details when I logged into my account.

item_themes: invoice data visible to wrong customer
output.key_quote: I could briefly see another customer's invoice details when I logged into my account

_targets: non-English input — labels and quotes stay in English for corpus-wide aggregation, even though the source text is Spanish
input: El soporte tarda demasiado en responder, ya llevo tres dias esperando una respuesta.

output.key_quote: tarda demasiado en responder, ya llevo tres dias esperando una respuesta

_targets: v2 addition: positive sentiment on a billing topic must still categorize as billing_pricing, not praise_positive -- category tracks TOPIC, sentiment tracks TONE. Directly targets the golden-set confusion where 'the new invoicing flow isn't nearly as bad as I feared' was mis-categorized as praise_positive.
input: Actually really pleased with how smooth the renewal invoice process was this time, no confusion at all.

output.key_quote: really pleased with how smooth the renewal invoice process was

_targets: v2 addition: a timeout/hang at scale with no error message is still performance (slowness/hanging), not a logic defect, even though the total silence can 'feel' broken. Directly targets the golden-set confusion on 'bulk-importing more than 500 rows just times out with no error'.
input: Uploading a batch of over 1000 records just hangs forever with no error message, works fine for smaller batches.

urgency_signals: large batch operations fail silently at scale
output.key_quote: just hangs forever with no error message

_targets: v2 addition: mild, lukewarm neutrality must NOT be read as genuine praise -- praise_positive requires actual enthusiasm or a clear positive endorsement, not merely the absence of complaint. Directly targets the golden-set confusion on 'it's fine I guess, does what it says'.
input: It's okay, does the job, nothing special either way.

output.key_quote: It's okay, does the job, nothing special either way.

_targets: v3 addition: generalizes the 'category=topic, not tone' rule beyond billing (v2 only had ONE example of this pattern, for billing_pricing) -- confirmed live across two separate golden-set eval runs that positive-toned-but-topical feedback about customer_support was still defaulting to praise_positive.
input: Huge shoutout to Jordan on the support team, walked me through a tricky migration and stayed on the call the whole time.

output.key_quote: walked me through a tricky migration and stayed on the call the whole time

_targets: v3 addition: a UI element that USED TO EXIST and disappeared entirely is a functional regression (bug_defect), not a discoverability/layout complaint (ui_ux). Directly targets a confusion that reproduced identically (same confidence score) across two separate golden-set eval runs on 'the export button disappeared'.
input: The share button that used to be in the top toolbar is just gone now, I can't find it anywhere.

urgency_signals: previously-working feature is now missing entirely
output.key_quote: the top toolbar is just gone now, I can't find it anywhere

_targets: v3 addition: urgency calibration -- when a workaround exists and the core task is still achievable, urgency stays medium, not high. Directly targets a systematic upward urgency bias observed consistently across three separate golden-set evaluation runs.
input: The bulk export feature is broken right now, but I can still export files one at a time as a workaround while it's fixed.

urgency_signals: does not block the underlying task
output.key_quote: I can still export files one at a time as a workaround while it's fixed
